-{"id":"gt-5wb7","title":"Update vision.md with proto/mol/wisp terminology","description":"Update the Steam Engine Metaphor section to use consistent phase terminology:

Current (vision.md):
- Proto molecules = fuel (templates)
- Wisps = steam (transient execution traces)  
- Digests = distillate (permanent records)

New terminology:
- **Proto** = crystal/solid - frozen template
- **Mol** = liquid - reified durable instance (tracked in git)
- **Wisp** = gas - ephemeral instance (evaporates after squash)
- **Digest** = distillate - compressed summary after squash

Key clarification needed:
- Proto → bond → creates either Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- The choice of Mol vs Wisp depends on --wisp flag
- Default is Mol (durable, recorded in main beads)
- Wisp lives in .beads-ephemeral/, squashes away","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:45.68234-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:37:08.968048-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wb7","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.307488-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-5wb7","title":"Update vision.md with proto/mol/wisp terminology","description":"Update the Steam Engine Metaphor section to use consistent phase terminology:

Current (vision.md):
- Proto molecules = fuel (templates)
- Wisps = steam (transient execution traces)  
- Digests = distillate (permanent records)

New terminology:
- **Proto** = crystal/solid - frozen template
- **Mol** = liquid - reified durable instance (tracked in git)
- **Wisp** = gas - ephemeral instance (evaporates after squash)
- **Digest** = distillate - compressed summary after squash

Key clarification needed:
- Proto → bond → creates either Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- The choice of Mol vs Wisp depends on --wisp flag
- Default is Mol (durable, recorded in main beads)
- Wisp lives in .beads-ephemeral/, squashes away","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:45.68234-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:42:37.672197-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:42:37.672197-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wb7","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.307488-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-5wtw","title":"Shutdown request handler","description":"Handle polecat shutdown requests:

When polecat runs 'gt handoff --shutdown':
1. Polecat sends mail to <rig>/witness requesting shutdown
2. Witness receives mail
3. Witness verifies:
   - Git working tree is clean
   - Work is submitted (MR exists or in queue)
   - No uncommitted beads changes
4. If clean:
   - gt session stop <rig>/<polecat>
   - git worktree remove polecats/<name>
   - git branch -d polecat/<name>
5. If not clean:
   - Send nudge back to polecat
   - Track retry count

Depends on fixing gt-dsfi (handoff deadlock).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:22.968711-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.300578-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-mxyj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:38.893061-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-5y5p","title":"Preflight molecule: verify baseline health before work","description":"Before assigning work, verify baseline (main branch) is healthy.

**From VC**: Self-healing state machine (HEALTHY → SELF_HEALING → ESCALATED). ~200 lines.

**Gas Town implementation**: Preflight molecule or refinery feature:
```yaml
preflight:
  gates: [test, lint, build]
  on_failure: create-fix-issue
```

Run at session start. If baseline broken, file a P0 fix issue and work on that first.

**Value**: Self-healing baseline. Agents don't start from broken state.

**VC lesson**: Prevents cascading failures. Agent shouldn't start work on broken code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:13.807641-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:13.807641-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5y5p","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.469804-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-g2d","title":"Mayor session cycling prompting","description":"Add session cycling section to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## When to Cycle

Cycle proactively when:
- Running for several hours
- Context feels crowded (losing track of earlier state)
- Major phase completed
- About to start complex new work

## Composing Handoff Notes

1. Gather information:
   town status    # Overall health
   town rigs      # Each rig state
   town inbox     # Pending messages
   bd ready       # Work items

2. Compose note with this structure:

[HANDOFF_TYPE]: mayor_cycle
[TIMESTAMP]: <current time>
[SESSION_DURATION]: <how long running>

## Active Swarms
<per-rig swarm status>

## Rig Status
<table of rig health>

## Pending Escalations
<issues needing your decision>

## In-Flight Decisions
<decisions being made>

## Recent Actions
<last 5-10 things you did>

## Delegated Work
<work sent to refineries>

## User Requests
<pending user asks>

## Next Steps
<what next session should do>

## Warnings/Notes
<critical info for next session>

3. Send handoff:
   town mail send mayor/ -s "Session Handoff" -m "<note>"

4. End session - next instance picks up from handoff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:26.188561-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:39.861022-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g2d","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.361163-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-g3zx","title":"Merge polecat/slit: docs bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Branch: polecat/slit

## Summary
Added comprehensive CLI reference documentation for the three molecule lifecycle commands to molecules.md:

- **bd mol bond**: Instantiate proto into Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- **bd mol squash**: Complete molecule and generate digest  
- **bd mol burn**: Abandon molecule without digest

Includes argument tables, behavior descriptions, examples, and a lifecycle diagram showing the steam engine metaphor mapping.

Closes: gt-odvf","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"merge-request","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:42:34.847015-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:42:34.847015-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-g44u","title":"Molecule Workflow Engine: Composable Crystallized Workflows","description":"# Epic: Molecule Workflow Engine

**Vision**: Molecules are crystallized, composable, nondeterministic-idempotent workflow templates. Any worker can pick up where any other worker was interrupted and continue along the molecule.

**Christmas Target**: Full molecule-based workflow engine operational by Dec 25, 2025.

## The Core Concepts

1. **Molecule**: Read-only workflow template (beads issue with type=molecule)
2. **Atom/Step**: Individual work unit with prose instructions
3. **Bond**: Dependency between steps
4. **Polymer/Derived**: Molecule composed from other molecules
5. **Instance**: Concrete beads created when molecule is attached to work

## Key Features Needed

### 1. Molecule Composition (Includes Directive)
Molecules can include other molecules:
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: gastown-polecat
Includes: mol-engineer-in-box

## Step: install-binary
Build and install the local gt binary.
Needs: submit
\`\`\`

### 2. Standard Molecules
- mol-install-go-binary: Single step to build/install gt
- mol-gastown-polecat: engineer-in-box + install-binary

### 3. Spawn Integration
\`gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>\` creates molecule instance then starts polecat on first ready step.

### 4. Nondeterministic Idempotence
- Steps are atomic (pending → in_progress → completed)
- Any worker can pick up any ready step
- Step timeout/recovery for stuck workers

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polecats can be spawned with mol-gastown-polecat
- [ ] Derived molecules work end-to-end
- [ ] 10+ polecat swarm completes molecule workflows
- [ ] install-go-binary step runs after successful merges","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:49:32.005023-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:23:08.857768-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:23:08.857768-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-g44u.1","title":"Molecule composition: Includes directive","description":"Add support for molecule composition via the Includes directive.

## Format
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: derived-name
Includes: mol-base-molecule

## Step: additional-step
Additional instructions here.
Needs: step-from-base
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Add \`Includes:\` parsing to ParseMoleculeSteps()
2. Resolve included molecule by ID
3. Merge steps from included molecule
4. Allow new steps to depend on included steps
5. Support multiple includes (polymers)

## Files to modify
- internal/beads/molecule.go
- internal/beads/molecule_test.go

## Acceptance
- [ ] Parse Includes directive
- [ ] Resolve and merge included steps
- [ ] Dependencies across molecules work
- [ ] Multiple includes supported
- [ ] Tests cover composition scenarios","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.981634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:55.055353-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:55.055353-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.1","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.983662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-g44u.2","title":"Create mol-install-go-binary molecule","description":"Create a single-step molecule for building and installing the gt binary.

## Molecule Definition
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: install-go-binary
Single step to rebuild and install the gt binary after code changes.

## Step: install
Build and install the gt binary locally.

Run from the rig directory:
\`\`\`
go build -o gt ./cmd/gt
go install ./cmd/gt
\`\`\`

Verify the installed binary is updated:
\`\`\`
which gt
gt --version  # if we have version command
\`\`\`
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Add to builtin_molecules.go
2. Update SeedBuiltinMolecules to include it
3. Run gt molecule seed

## Acceptance
- [ ] mol-install-go-binary exists in beads
- [ ] Can be instantiated standalone
- [ ] Can be included by other molecules","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:11.178225-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:02:36.758908-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:02:36.758908-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.2","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:11.180129-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
